text: Trapped in Trinumvira
8 ABY (RL dates: April 9th, 1999)
Shenneret Veery
Caspar
Jonathan Webb, Azya McTavish, Whitney, Heinrich Stalh
roster: Jonathan Webb, Azya McTavish, Whitney, Heinrich Stalh

text: Caspar Democratic Union, Galactic Empire, New Republic

War has come to Caspar, and up until this particular day, Shenneret Veery has been content with joining forces with her friends at the Sandbar to try to assist civilians whose homes have been destroyed in making it south to the somewhat more secure refuge of Trinumvira Base. That was until she was told one too many times to her face that she was too young to be facing the conflict that has swept over the planet. And so, driven not only by her annoyance over her age -- for hadn't far younger individuals fought in the Rebellion? -- but also defiant and determined to give something back in kind to the forces responsible for the death of Rekkie Sheldon, Shen has seized the opportunity to join in a battle in the middle of Plaxton. She's succeeded in killing a handful of the Imperial enemy, but she's gotten herself wounded for her trouble, not to mention discovered by the comrades of the soldier whose gear she's stolen. With the wounding of General Veers by none other than Shenner's own friend Jonathan Webb, and the trapping of Caspar's forces inside Trinumvira Base, the Imperial forces have backed off for the time being. And Webb's platoon is about to report to their commander on the intruder in their midst...
<pre>

Webb's brow furrows as he notices the line of Imperial forces begin to%r%tretreat. Still squeezing off shots at any targets of opportunity, he grimly%r%treports into his comlink, "Trinum command, this is Halo lead at point%r%tOrange-7. Imperial forces to the north seem to be in retreat. Repeat,%r%treporting Imperial retreat..." he closes the channel to allow him to%r%tcontrol his breathing enough to squeeze off one last shot, between breaths,%r%tbetween heartbeats, striking an ST officer in the back. To his left, a%r%tsoldier manning a high powered electro-optical sensor slaved to a target%r%tdesignator reports, "Sir.... umm.... I think you shot General Veers?!

"I know," comes Webb's cold, calm answer as he starts to rise to his feet to%r%tturn to face the forces that have rallied about him. He lets out a long%r%tsigh, then shakes his head, and re-slings his rifle to begin to aid in the%r%ttask of collecting the wounded. Now his hands shake, though only a tiny%r%ttremor.

Now, at last, Webb's platoon can risk turning some attention to the problem%r%tthat has sprung up in their midst. "Sir!" comes a shout from nearby, as a%r%tpair of soldiers who had been fighting to either side of the slender figure%r%tin the gear of Sergeant Sung seize the chance to detain her. "Sir, if%r%tthey're retreating, we got a problem over here you need to see!"

Shenner hisses involuntarily as the bigger soldier to her left side prods her%r%tleft arm, setting off a flare of sensation that seems to slice from her%r%tshoulder down to her fingertips and back again. _How the hell--_ Then she%r%thas no time to think of anything further, as the Marine to her right barks,%r%t"Okay, _drop it_!" Knowing better than to argue with that tone, Shenner%r%treleases her hold on her rifle. Its stock is bloody as it drops down to the%r%tearth before her; so, for that matter, is her left arm.

Webb eyes Shenner for a moment, then looks out over the field of Imperial%r%tbodies and burned out vehicles before he asks of the two soldiers who are%r%tattempting to detain Shenner, "How'd she do?" His sensor goggles zoom in%r%tclose upon the field of death... many of the Imperials out there still%r%tmove. Perhaps when darkness falls, he can equip a team with thermal%r%tcamouflage and Ghillie, and go out hunting in the field. Now, it's time to%r%tbreath a little, and thank whatever higher power you believe in that you're%r%tstill alive. Webb isn't a religious man. Instead, he silently thanks those%r%tliving and dead who made him what he is. Lifting his sensor visor, then%r%tremoving his helmet, he peers back towards the 'soldier', studying her%r%tintently, as he reaches out to undo the attachment points upon Shenner's%r%thelmet before finally lifting it off of the woman's head.

Green eyes. Dishevelled russet hair pulled back from slender features in a%r%tbraid down the back of her head. Features drawn into lines of pain and%r%tincreasing weariness.... it is, in short, Shenner, and as the helmet is%r%tpulled off her, taking with it the tactical readouts she'd been ignoring%r%tbecause their information makes no sense to her, she swallows hard and%r%tbraces herself for the consequences she'd been ignoring before.

"She seemed to know how to handle her rifle, sir," reports the Marine to the%r%tmusician's right, "but we realized something was wrong when she didn't take%r%tcommand of the heavy artillery. And when she spoke, sir."
The soldier to Shen's left adds, peering critically at the redhead's arm,%r%t"She's taken a hit."

Webb arches an eyebrow faintly as the familiar features emerge from beneath%r%tthe helmet that he removes from the woman who wears Gunnery Sergeant Sung's%r%tarmor. The weary, dishevelled Ranger faces the wounded bard for a long%r%tmoment, wondering for a moment how she ended up fighting beside him in the%r%tuniform and gear of a Marine. The stresses of combat can produce somewhat%r%todd reactions from individuals, especially after the battle is over and one%r%tfinds time returning to its normal pace, and one's body is jittery with%r%tebbing adrenaline. Still, Webb's response to the sight of his friend is%r%tperhaps a little odd even by those standards. He simply steps forwards,%r%twraps his arms snugly about the musician-turned-soldier's waist, taking%r%tcare not to put any undue pressure upon the woman's wounded arm, and lifts%r%ther from her feet to twirl her about. Even if you couldn't hear him%r%tlaughing, you could tell that right now he's rather happy to see you.

Paul would have shouted at her. She's absolutely certain that Ariani'll%r%tshout, too, and possibly Karm, though volume isn't the habit of either%r%tTethra or Aa'leet. And so Shenner was prepared for shouting and lectures,%r%teven for Webb and his compatriots hauling her off and slapping her into the%r%tbrig by way of disciplining her for this little stunt of hers. What she%r%t_wasn't_ prepared for was being scooped up off her feet and spun around.%r%tShenner's world lurches a little, dizziness spiking up behind her eyes as%r%ther body starts informing her sharply that it has issues with the abuse her%r%tarm has taken. "Uh... hi," she croaks uncertainly, managing a wan lopsided%r%tgrin while the two Marines who had called Webb over gape at their CO.

Webb finally sets Shenner lightly back onto her feet, and after another long%r%tmoment, releases her from the hug altogether. Webb is, after all, not about%r%tto yell at a certain impetuous bard for something that he'd probably have%r%tdone himself, if he were in her shoes. Besides... given the present%r%tsituation, Trinumvira's defenders will probably need her. To the two%r%tMarines who had been restraining Shenner, he says, "This woman is now%r%tentitled to the brevet rank of Private First Class, with the privileges%r%tand responsibilities associated with that rank, and service in the Marine%r%tCorps of the Caspar Democratic Union, if she chooses to accept," noting the%r%tlook on the faces of the two Marines, Webb adds to that statement, "Just%r%ttry to find an officer on this bloody post that'll stop me! Get her below,%r%tand get her treated."

Now it's Shenner's turn to gape. "Wha?" is all she manages to get out, as her%r%tjaw drops and she's set more or less down on her feet. Her? A Marine? The%r%twhirling her head's already started threatening to do in reaction to the%r%tincreasing wetness along her arm picks up pace, and she looks startledly%r%tfrom face to face as the Marine to her right takes her arm. "This way,"%r%tshe's told briskly, and she succeeds in a dazed nod. The other Marine gapes%r%tharder at Webb; this is NOT the response one generally expects when someone%r%tswipes a soldier's gear and uses it for unauthorized entrance into a%r%tbattlefield. But he's not about to argue with Webb, not right now, and%r%tbetween the two of them they get the bard and her weaponry hustled off into%r%tthe base.

Webb eyes the Marine who gapes at him, fixing the man with his steely grey%r%teyes, eyes that are settling into the 'thousand yard stare' as levels of%r%tadrenaline rush are steadily stripped away and fatigue slips in, "You%r%tweren't in the Rebellion, were you, Marine?" As he turns to follow the%r%tmarine who escorts Shenner back down into the tunnel leading to the core of%r%tthe base, he adds, "I think Standard Operating Procedure just shot to%r%tribbons..."%rWebb heads into the dark tunnel, and disappears from sight.
Webb has left.%rYou stride into the tunnel, minding the dark..
.%r.%r.%r.%r.%r.%r.%r.%r.%r.%r.%r.%r.%rThe tunnel finally spills into a small parking area within the rock, and a%r%tlarge doorway leads into...

Muster Room - Trinumvira Base%r%tLooking like an attempt to civilize a small cave, this room not only%r%tis the official assembly and briefing room, but the recreation hall and%r%tlounge during off-hours for the troops of Caspar. Unit emblems coat the%r%twalls, hanging next to holoscreens and mission boards that never seem to%r%tidle. Chairs and tables are scattered about, often crammed with soldiers or%r%tbase personnel. On the far side, a blue-lit corridor leads down a ramp%r%ttowards what sounds to be like ship engines and machinery. On one side, a%r%ttight staircase spirals up, on the opposite, it spirals down.

%t-=-=-=-=-=-=<>=-=-=-=-=-=-%r=> Webb%r=> Stalh%r=> Azya%r=> Dlasik%r=> Weapon Locker(#7967V)%r%t-=-=-=-=-=<>=-=-=-=-=-%r%torridor leads to Brig - Trinumvira Base.%r%townstairs leads to Medical Facility - Trinumvira Base.%r%tpstairs leads to Military Operations Center (MOPS'C) - Trinumvira Base.%r%t

assageway leads to Trinum Landing Bay - Trinumvira Base.%r%tut leads to Virabluff Yards - Trinumvira Base.
Azya looks at you for a moment.

Flanked by a pair of rather startled-looking Marines, herself in combat gear%r%tand with a bloodied left arm, a pale and fatigued Shenner is hustled into%r%tview from outside. The two soldiers escorting her are also carrying her%r%tweaponry, a pistol and a rifle with a slight smear of blood on the stock,%r%tand the three are obviously bound for the medical facilities.

Azya is walking slowly across the area, guarded by a slightly jumpy Gunnery%r%tSargeant Sung. The Imperial doesn't seem to be of a mind to go anywhere in%r%tparticular, except away from the quarters she's been locked in all%r%tafternoon.
Whitney comes down the length of the tunnel.%rWhitney has arrived.

Webb trudges into the Muster room, escorting a wounded, russet-haired woman%r%twho is attired in body armor that would normally be in the possession of%r%tone Gunnery Sergeant Ilyana Sung. Another Marine flanks the woman in%r%tquestion, and others escort or carry comrades in various wounded states.%r%tThe defense of Trinumvira, which while all too costly for the Empire, was%r%tobviously not without cost to the Marines, judging by the steady trail of%r%twounded and bodies that are being brought in. Knowing that the medical%r%tfacilities are bound to be clogged, Webb gestures towards the muster room,%r%t"Corpsmen can work on the less serious cases in here... Medical bay is%r%treporting that they're running pretty close to full as is." At times like%r%tthis, he's glad that Trinumvira was built with plenty of room. Technically,%r%tthe true depths of the tunnels is a bit of a state secret. Perhaps this has%r%tprompted some to refer to this place as a 'Vrelt-hole straight to hell'. To%r%tShenner he gestures, "Here... sit," before he begins to remove the load%r%tbearing torso-shell of the woman's armor.

Azya stops suddenly in her wanderings as she suddenly becomes aware of the%r%tsteady stream of incoming wounded and bodies. She blinks, her face%r%tobviously torn between these signs of cost to the CDU and her natural%r%tsympathy. Hrm...perhaps that would explain why she joined the Navy and not%r%tthe Bureau or COMPNOR. She watches the parade of sorts quietly, if keenly,%r%tmaking no move to help or hinder those aiding the wounded.

Shenner hadn't quite yet realized exactly how _heavy_ a soldier's gear is,%r%tuntil Webb encourages her to stop and sit. As her two escorts back off,%r%tleaving her to their CO's... er... graces, the musician more or less sags%r%tdown into a sitting position where she's bid. Once she does, she rasps%r%ttiredly to Webb, "I was gonna bring the -- ow! -- gear back..."

Whitney carries a wounded child in, no expression in those violet eyes as she%r%tcarefully hands the child over towards the medics. The child's arm was%r%tripped off right at the shoulder, having been used as a shield by the%r%tImperial troopers. Violet eyes turn to quietly stare at the Imperial%r%tsoldier in the room as her lips compressed into a thin line. Muscles%r%ttwitched as she gripped her armored covered fist before she moves towards%r%tthe injured personnel and attempted to help with what medical expertise she%r%thas, doing so meticulously and rigidly.

Stalh too, his eyes cold and showing none of the warmth a compassionate%r%tmember of the Republic should show, scans the scene of desolation before%r%thim. Some fallen show fake pride and honour in repelling the Imperial%r%tmenace... for now. Shaking his head, he starts to move away in disgust -%r%tthe thought of Azya gone from his mind for a moment. False glimmers of%r%thope. Ones that will soon be snuffed out like a candle in the wind. The%r%tdeath knell for the CDU was nigh, Stalh thought. Nothing he or anyone else%r%tfor that matter would be able to stop it. Prolong it yes, but no one can%r%tever hold off death for ever. It will be ruthless. He expects nothing else,%r%tnor wants nothing else. Only the anger of a heated fued of sadistic%r%tproportions excites him now. And it is perhaps the wailing keen of a dying%r%tman that makes Stalh smile. Death. It was around him. Ironic, really. In%r%tthis place, on this planet he hates so much, he has found home.

The catches for the torso shell are located under each arm. Once Webb has%r%topened them, the torso shell of Shenner's field armor opens up like a%r%tclamshell, thanks to hinged, semirigid sections atop the shoulders.%r%tCarefully, he lifts off the armor, and now begins to examine Shenner's arm,%r%tafter retrieving Shen's field medical pack from the woman's load bearing%r%tgear.

Azya turns slowly, watching the scenes around her unfold. Some of what she%r%tsees apparently causes her pity to win over for moments, while at other%r%ttimes, it appears that her satisfaction? Pride? Loyalty? for the Empire's%r%thaving taken such a cost wins. She keeps her hands by her side, the circles%r%tunder her eyes, and somewhat healed cut and bruised left temple definitely%r%tnot meriting any notice among this...in fact, if anything, the pilot is%r%tavoided. While all other able bodies are recruited to treat the wounded,%r%tthe Imperial pilot is avoided like the plague, leaving a small circle of%r%t"No man's land" around herself and Sung.

Reaching out from within one of the pockets from her armor, Whitney draws out%r%ta strange amulet...the one that Azya had seen her with before the battle.%r%tShe stares at it for a long moment holding it in her hand before she%r%tpresses it to her lips and slips it around her neck. She approached Webb,%r%tpausing before him as she kneels down to check the extent of Shenner's%r%tinjuries, "Where does it hurt?" She asked as she prods the area of the%r%twound gently.

Ouch. Shenner must have taken a bit of shrapnel from that explosion that had%r%tgone off near her; her sleeve is shredded up there around her bicep, a%r%tsingle tear through which a small shard of metal has managed to lodge. It's%r%tnot big wound. Nor is it bleeding too heavily at this point... but it's%r%tbeen bleeding, slowly but surely, enough to have sent trickles of blood%r%tdown to her hand by now and dampen her sleeve on the way. Shenner peers%r%tdown at her own arm with a trace of bemusement darkening her eyes. And at%r%tWhitney's approach and the exploration of her injury, she sucks in a sharp%r%tgasp and croaks, "That'd be it, right there..."

Whitney takes the kit out from Webb's hand casting a look at him that clearly%r%tsaid, "Men...they don't know a thing about medical care." as she opens the%r%tbox and peers inside. The medical supplies in the kit were adequate as she%r%treaches out to rip the sleeve off from Shenner. She turns to Shenner, "this%r%tis going to sting." as she pours an antiseptic solution around the wound.%r%tShe ties a piece of cloth above the wound and tightens it to stop the blood%r%tflow. Carefully she uses a pair of tweezers and slowly ease the shrapnel%r%tout from its wound. The blood oozes from the open wound as Whitney cleans%r%tit using the antiseptic lotion before wrapping it up using the bandage. She%r%tunties the cloth above the bicep, "rest that." She states curtly as she%r%tturns to replace the equipment back in the kit.

Circling like a bird of prey, Stalh's attention has now drifted back to that%r%tof the Imperial pilot. A captive. A disgrace. Be it she be Republic,%r%tEmpire, CDU or just a down on her luck pirate, there was no excuse for%r%tbeing captured. Death before dishonour. Isn't that what the Empire had%r%ttaught him? Perhaps some of his lessons on Carida and again in the squadron%r%t'Whirlwind', were of good use to him. Drawing near her, but wary of the%r%tGunnery Sergeant, he sends a haunting whisper in her direction:
%t"What are fears but voices Airy,%r%tWhispering harm where harm is not.%r%tAn deluding the unwary,%r%tUntil the fatal bolt is shot...
"Greetings from the Republic, Azya." he says with a chilling laugh that%r%tflitters above the nervous voices of those near the man with the Imperial%r%ttattoo.

Webb lets out a long sigh as he is able to turn the task of treating Shenner%r%tover to Whitney. It's probably about time too... even through the%r%tcamouflaged face paint that he wears, it begins to show through. As usual%r%the's dirty, he's bloody (though probably not with his own blood, and sore.%r%tHe removes an old, coil bound paper log-book from one pocket, and takes a%r%tmoment to update it, including a count of confirmed kills, probable kills,%r%tand enemy WIAs that he's inflicted, and a record of anything significant%r%tthat he's damaged. If he weren't fighting a great suicidal wave, this tally%r%twould probably be the envy of any soldier, but killing men who have no%r%tparticular sense of self-preservation is like... the phrase 'clubbing baby%r%tnerfs' pops into his mind. He trudges his way towards Stalh, and Azya... to%r%tthe Republic 'diplomat', he states flatly, "Heinrich," before shifting his%r%tgaze to Azya and Gunny Sung.

Sting... well, that's one word for it. Shenner lets out a louder hiss,%r%tthrough tightly clenched teeth, as Whitney efficiently attends to her arm.%r%t_You can handle this, right, street rat? It's just a little scratch. Not%r%tthe first time you've been hurt. And hey, coulda been worse, this coulda%r%tbeen Mandalore..._ Her thoughts start to scatter, green gaze going a little%r%tunfocused. Battle fatigue and the jittery drain of adrenaline from an%r%tovercharged system can strike veterans like Webb, and Shenner is no%r%tveteran. With her free hand she surreptitiously tries to prop herself up%r%twhere she sits, just barely managing to keep sitting up reasonably%r%tstraight. "I can do that," she whispers hoarsely to Whitney's order.

Azya spins on her heal at the sound of the soft, yet familiar, voice%r%tspeaking to her. Her eyes widen slightly in surprise as she see's Stalh.%r%tUnconsciously, the pilot lifts her arms, wrapping them protectively around%r%ther mid-section as she takes a small, half-step backwards. Death before%r%tdishonor, perhaps...but things change when there is more than one life in%r%tthe balance, and it's impossible to tell how it will effect someone until%r%tit does. She narrows her eyes slightly at the man she considers more of an%r%tagent than a diplomat.

Whitney nods as she leans forward to assist Shenner a little, the amulet%r%tdangling from her neck to reveal a round circle and within were a pair of%r%tpraying hands. Strange for it to be on a solider. She straightens again as%r%tshe cast a look towards the confrontation that seems to be brewing from%r%tAzya's direction. Briefly her eyes move over the man Webb called "Heinrich"%r%t, the violet eyes narrowing as she eyes the tattoo on his arm. Her lips%r%tthinned again as she presses them firmly together as she contemplates her%r%tchoices, her gaze flicking from Azya to Stalh and back again.

"Changed sides once again?" he snorts. An agent? Perhaps. Often his master,%r%tthe dark and her vile tendencies to corrupt the young and innocent, have%r%tcalled upon his services to desecrate yet another soul in the perpetual%r%tdestruction of all that is good and sweet. Such feelings deserved no place%r%tin this cold brutal world such as his. It was mockery of some higher power%r%tthat things even existed - to torment and torture. Very few beings knew the%r%texistence of such things, and, Stalh thinks while placing a small smirk%r%tthat kisses his lips like poison, it was those that deserved to perish the%r%tmost. Tracer was all but his to control. Blaze too, was yet another minion.%r%tSoon, he starts as he smiles towards the woman, she will be his too.

Quite abruptly, Shenner is seized by a ridiculous desire to sing again. But%r%tthis time, it's not the defiant lyrics of the "March of Tanaab"; no, it's%r%tthe irreverent impromptu words some drunken wit once thought up to be sung%r%tto the tune of the Imperial March. _We are the Empire, we're big, and we're%r%tmean / We are Darth Vader's destruction machine...._ Just barely, she%r%tmanages to stop herself from babble-singing the words, even under her%r%tbreath. _What the kark's wrong with me?_ she wonders blearily, reaching for%r%tthe silver-and-brass necklace she hadn't removed in putting on Sung's gear.%r%tMomentarily left to herself, she quietly reels.

Azya continues to eye Stalh wearily through narrowed eyes. She didn't trust%r%tthis man before, when he 'skimmed' her mail over her shoulder, or when%r%tMorganna warned her of him, and she doesn't trust him now. She spares a%r%tmomentary glance at Webb, her note to detail quick in picking up his%r%ttiredness, and how highly stressed he is at the moment. Her gaze reverts%r%tback to Stalh almost immediately though, and remains there, watching him%r%tsilently.

The LCP takes that opportunity to approach the trio. Her gaze fixated on that%r%tman's tattoo as she does so. She turns to Webb and ask quietly, "Is that%r%tanother prisoner? Should I escort him to the Brig? Perhaps he'll feel much%r%tbetter there."

Webb steps up to Azya's side, gazing sternly across at Stalh with his steely%r%tgrey eyes. The Ranger's Imperial Nova tattoo shows quite clearly through the%r%tsmudged camouflage paint. This is, perhaps, the first time that the two%r%thave engaged in conversation of any sort, or confronted one another this%r%tclosely. And yet, Webb always refers to the man as 'Heinrech', not whatever%r%tfanciful title he's officially supposed to call a pissant, sadistic%r%tdiplomat. "The two of you know each other?" Something in Webb's stance%r%timplies a certain... protectiveness of Azya.

Stalh%rThe Human occupying your line of vision radiates a sense of self-control. His%r%t185 (6ft) stands out, more noticeably perhaps due to his dominating%r%tcomposure of control and rigidness. His body appears well toned, more so%r%tthan normal due to the fact that the man is wearing a tight hugging black%r%ttee-shirt, which shows off the curvaceous nature of his well built arms.%r%tThe tee-shirt is plain black and void of any markings. The shirt is tucked%r%tneatly into pants which are commonly known as 'urban fatigues'. These%r%tpants are grey, white and gun-metal blue, which make them possible to be%r%thidden better against the dull grey colour of most civilised cities. The%r%tpants are tucked tightly into highly polished black combat boots, which%r%tstop just about 5 centimetres above the ankle. The man's face marks an%r%tadded feature to his already military appearance. His skin is a nordic tan%r%tthat glows in the light. Coupled with this is a head of dark brown hair.%r%tHis eyes are captivating. They are the darkest green possibly imagined -%r%talmost black. Hauntingly they stare, the eyes appear unnatural - like a%r%tshield blocking off prying eyes to his soul - almost demonical.%rHis hands, unaccustomedly ungloved, reveal a single black mark on the wrists:%r%tST_SS80826. Another tattoo is covered by his sleeve, it appears blue, but%r%tone cannot be certain - intriguing it is however.
%t-=-=-=-=-=-=<<Carried Virtual Objects>>=-=-=-=-=-=-%r=> DY-255 Heavy Blaster Pistol%r=> Protective Vest <Worn>%r=> MX Heavy Blaster Pistol

His eyes remain on her for a moment before bowing slightly in nothing less%r%tthan a condescending manner. "No greeting for your former comrade?" he%r%tdrawls mockingly, his cold face cracking slightly as his eyes set upon the%r%tstarburst so artistically burned upon the man's forehead. He shows no%r%tsurprise though, as if he has often seen that tactic used before in his own%r%tcruel sessions with unfortunate soldiers who had crossed his wrath or the%r%tEmperor's grace when he was once one of their number. "New fashion from%r%tCoruscant?" he asks innocently, though his eyes betray the laughter%r%tcoursing within him like the river of blood that seems to flow all over the%r%tsurface of the planet. "It is... different, to say the least." His smile,%r%twhich crept up over the course of his mocking acknowledgement of Webb,%r%tsours slightly at the audacity of the Lance Corporal now before him. "Would%r%ta prisoner of war have two heavy blaster pistols?" His look at utter%r%tdisbelief of the stupidity of this soldier is clearly apparent. "CDU%r%tStandards are seeming to improve." Cold and cutting, particularly as Stalh%r%tlooks around at the dead and dying near him.

Azya nods once, curtly, as she answers Webb's question quietly. "We have met,%r%tonce or twice." She can't help but smirk slightly at the implications of%r%tWhitney's question, but, at Stalh's reply, and the thought of what he's%r%tsurely done in the past with such..instruments, her arms tighten around her%r%tmiddle slightly. She quickly lapses back into silence.

Apparently , with her concentration fixated on the tattoo, she did not%r%tnoticed the two blasters around his waist. Shrugging her shoulders, she%r%tstates clearly, "perhaps then you'll like to hand those arms over , in case%r%tyou "misuse" them." as her violet eyes glints clearly as she looks as%r%tthough she would enjoy taking it off him...and not so gently.

Webb shakes his head at Whitney's inquiry, and actually emits a faint%r%tchuckle, "No need for that, Lance Corporal. Can't tell the brig apart from%r%tany other room in this force-forsaken vrelt-hole to hell any ways." He does%r%thave a point... it's all the same bloody cave, or grave, depending upon%r%tyour point of view. Somehow he suspects that just being stuck down here%r%twith the Empire outside, and Whitney feigning ignorance, and Shenner being%r%tmusical will invariably drive the former Imperial up the wall.
He chuckles faintly at Stalh's remark, and shakes his head, "Oh, I was never%r%tyour comrade, Heinrech. Product of the same machine, perhaps... /radically/%r%tdifferent process."
Webb's cold gaze, would be quite familiar, by the way, to someone who often%r%twatches IGN. Uh... didn't this guy kinda kidnap the female pilot who he now%r%tseems so protective of?

_Get a grip, street rat,_ Shenner orders herself sternly. marshalling her%r%tthoughts into something resembling cohesive order. _Can't just fall asleep%r%tright here..._ Even _if_ sleep sounds like a very good idea. A marvellously%r%tgood idea. The best idea she's heard in a month of weekends. She manages to%r%tfocus her glassy gaze on a sweep around the room, interested in discovering%r%twhere Webb has gone; when she spots him, though, and the suspiciously%r%tfamiliar figure near him, fatigue suddenly takes a back seat to a new surge%r%tof adrenaline. Is that who she _thinks_ it is? Propelled by that surge, she%r%tforces herself to her feet.
